edges.description: Section B lists regulatory conformance criteria, including quality assurance and seismic design, which are prerequisites for determining the design life of plant modules discussed in Section A.

Section A references regulatory conformance criteria for safety-related structures, while Section B describes the Central Utility Building as a nonsafety-related structure, indicating that the CUB is not subject to the design life requirements governed by the regulatory guides cited in Section A.

Section A discusses regulatory conformance for safety-related structures, systems, and components (SSCs), while Section B describes the Central Utility Building as a nonsafety-related structure, indicating that the CUB is not subject to the regulatory criteria outlined in Section A.

Section A provides nominal design parameters for the NuScale Power Module, while Section B outlines the module's conformance with regulatory guides, connecting them through the regulatory requirements that the design parameters must satisfy.

Section A provides nominal design parameters for the NuScale Power Module, while Section B details the module's conformance with regulatory guides, both of which are necessary to determine if the design life (a design parameter) meets regulatory requirements.
Section B lists regulatory conformance criteria, including quality assurance and seismic design, which are prerequisites for determining the design life of plant structures described in Section A.
Section B lists regulatory conformance criteria, including quality assurance and seismic design, which are prerequisites for determining the design life of plant structures described in Section A.
Section B lists regulatory conformance criteria, including quality assurance and seismic design, which are prerequisites for determining the design life of plant components discussed in Section A.
Section B lists regulatory conformance criteria, including quality assurance and seismic design, which are prerequisites for determining the design life of plant modules discussed in Section A.

Section B references regulatory conformance criteria, including quality assurance and seismic design, which are prerequisites for establishing the design life of safety-related structures described in Section A.

Section B references regulatory guides and quality assurance criteria that include requirements for design life, which Section A does not address; thus, Section B provides the regulatory context relevant to determining the design life of the NuScale Power Module.

Section B references regulatory guides, including those related to quality assurance and seismic design, which set requirements that Section A's described design features (such as modularization and passive safety) must satisfy, thereby connecting the design characteristics to regulatory expectations that influence design life.
Section B references regulatory guides that include quality assurance and design criteria, which set requirements such as design life that Section A's operating characteristics must satisfy.

Section A outlines the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified NuScale Power Module design and site-specific elements, while Section B describes the functions and contents of the Central Utility Building, which is part of the site-specific design referenced in Section A.

Section A outlines the interface requirements and boundaries of the certified design, while Section B details how the design conforms to regulatory guides, both providing foundational context for determining the design life of the NuScale Power Module by specifying what is included in the certified design and how it meets regulatory expectations.

Section A outlines the interface requirements and boundaries of the certified design, while Section B details the conformance of the design with regulatory guides, both providing context for what aspects of the NuScale Power Module's design life are governed by certification and regulatory compliance.

Section B describes the operating characteristics and modular design of the NuScale Power Module, which are influenced by the engineered safety features and containment vacuum benefits detailed in Section A, connecting the design approach to operational reliability and longevity relevant to design life.

Section B references Section A by stating that Section 1.2 (from Section A) identifies the structures, systems, and components included in the certified design, thereby connecting the general plant description to the interface requirements.

Section B references Section A by stating that Section 1.2 (from Section A) identifies the structures, systems, and components included in the certified design, thereby connecting the general plant description to the interface requirements.

Section A explains that the FSAR uses technical guidance from the DSRS and SRP as the basis for the NuScale design and refers to Section 1.9 for detailed evaluation of conformance, while Section B (Table 1.9-2) provides the actual conformance evaluation with regulatory guides, thus Section A references and points the reader to the detailed information found in Section B.

Section A explains that the FSAR uses the DSRS and SRP as the basis for the NuScale design and refers to Section 1.9 for detailed conformance evaluation, while Section B (Table 1.9-2) provides the actual conformance details with regulatory guides, thus Section A references and introduces the content found in Section B.

Section A explains that the FSAR uses the DSRS and SRP as the technical basis for the NuScale design and refers to Section 1.9 for detailed conformance, while Section B (Table 1.9-2) provides the actual conformance evaluation with regulatory guides, thus Section A references and points the reader to the detailed information found in Section B.

Section A explains that the FSAR uses the DSRS and SRP as the technical basis for the NuScale design and that conformance with these standards is evaluated in Section 1.9, while Section B (Table 1.9-2) provides the detailed conformance evaluation with specific regulatory guides, thus Section A references and introduces the detailed content found in Section B.

Section A lists input parameters and initial conditions for accident analyses, while Section B outlines conformance with regulatory guides including quality assurance and seismic design, both referencing the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report and its regulatory compliance but without directly addressing or specifying the design life of the module.

Section A lists conformance with high-level regulatory standards (SRP/DSRS), while Section B details conformance with specific Regulatory Guides, both providing evidence of how the NuScale design meets regulatory expectations relevant to design life.

Section A lists general conformance with regulatory criteria (SRP/DSRS) without specific acceptance criteria for design life, while Section B details conformance with specific Regulatory Guides such as RG 1.28 (Quality Assurance) and RG 1.29 (Seismic Design), which may include requirements or expectations related to design life, thus Section B provides specification details that could inform the general conformance stated in Section A.

Section A describes the general structure and referencing approach of the FSAR, while Section B provides a specific example of how the FSAR demonstrates conformance with DSRS requirements, showing that Section A's general guidance is applied in Section B's detailed content.

Section A explains that the FSAR uses the DSRS and SRP as the basis for the NuScale design and refers to Section 1.9 for a detailed evaluation of conformance, while Section B (Table 1.9-3) provides the actual conformance evaluation with the SRP and DSRS, thus Section A references Section B for supporting details.

Section A states that the FSAR preparation used the DSRS as technical guidance and that conformance with the DSRS is evaluated in Section 1.9, while Section B provides specific details of conformance with DSRS 15.5.1-15.5.2 in Section 1.9, thus Section A references and sets up the context for the detailed evaluations found in Section B.

Section A explains that Section 1.9 provides a detailed evaluation of conformance with regulatory standards, while Section B (Table 1.9-3) presents the actual conformance evaluation, thus Section A references and introduces the content found in Section B.

Section A discusses specific DSRS sections and their conformance status, while Section B provides a broader table summarizing conformance with various SRP and DSRS sections, including those referenced in Section A, thus Section A provides details/specifications for the general conformance overview in Section B.
